---
title: "Alexis Weber, Ph.D."
subtitle: "Curriculum Vitae"
title-block-banner: "media/backgrnd4.png" 
format:
  html:
    css: cv.css  
---

<a id="top"></a>

[Download CV (PDF)](assets/cv/Alexis_Weber_CV.pdf){.btn .btn-primary .mb-3}

## Human Genetics Computational Scientist

[Email](mailto:alexisweber@g.ucla.edu) · [GitHub](https://github.com/alexisweber)

## Education

**Ph.D., Human Genetics** — University of California, Los Angeles

**B.Sc., Genomics and Molecular Genetics** — Michigan State University

## Certifications

-   Certificate in Fundamentals of AI/ML in Precision Medicine — Stanford University
-   Certificate in Fundamentals of Data Science in Precision Medicine and Cloud Computing— Stanford University
-   Entering Mentorship Training Certificate — UCLA

## Publications

-   Weber A., *et al.* “A single cell multi-omic analysis identifies molecular and gene-regulatory mechanisms dysregulated in the developing Down syndrome neocortex.” **Science** (2025). \[Link\]

# Projects

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Ts21 Atlas — in vivo</strong></summary>

-   🔗 Shiny app: <https://YOURAPPURL>
-   📦 GitHub: <https://github.com/alexisweber/Ts21_Neocortex_singleNucleiMultiome>
-   📄 Publication: <https://doi.org/xxxx>
-   🧩 Submodules:
    -   Cell–cell communication — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/cellchat-compare>
    -   Pseudotime — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/pseudotime-pipeline>
    -   Disease enrichment — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/disease-enrichment>
    -   Trisomy analysis — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/trisomy-21-analytics>

</details>

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Ts21 Atlas — in vitro</strong></summary>

-   🔗 Shiny app: <https://YOURAPPURL>
-   📦 GitHub: <https://github.com/alexisweber/Ts21_phNPC_singleNucleiMultiome>
-   📄 Publication: <https://doi.org/xxxx>
-   🧩 Submodules:
    -   Pseudotime — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/invitro-pseudotime>
    -   Gene Ontology — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/go-enrichment>
    -   Differential expression — 📦 <https://github.com/alexisweber/de-analysis>

</details>

## Skills

### Languages

Python · R · Bash

### Data Analysis

Machine learning · Multiome · Biostatistics

### Scientific Specializations

Genetics & Genomics · Neuroscience · Neurodevelopment

### Experimental Lab Work

Single-nuclei multiomic prep & sequencing · Nuclei isolation · Primary human neural progenitor cell culture ·\
Immunohistochemistry / Immunocytochemistry · DNA cloning/extraction/seq/analysis · RNA extraction/seq/analysis

## Research Experience

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Graduate Student Researcher</strong></summary>

University of California, Los Angeles — Defining Molecular Dysregulation in Down Syndrome Neocortex and Neural Progenitor Cells - Single cell multiomic (RNA and ATAC) Sequencing - Multiomic gene regulatory network computational analysis - Cortical and neocortical nuclei isolation - Primary human neural progenitor cell culture and analysis

</details>

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Researcher</strong></summary>

Van Andel Research Institute - Identify Epigenetic Aberrations Underlying Tau Pathology in Alzheimer’s Disease Patients - Bulk RNA-Sequencing and Analysis - ChIP-Seq and Hi-C Preparation and Analysis

-   Characterize Mutations Associated with Cellular Trafficking Pathology in Familial Parkinson’s Disease
    -   Primary neuronal cell culture
    -   Genetic engineering and cloning
    -   In vitro Parkinson’s disease modeling

</details>

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Undergraduate Researcher</strong></summary>

Michigan State University Determine the role of alternative RNA editing in Trypanosoma brucei associated with African Sleeping Sickness - Develop pipelines for RNA editing analyses - Develop pipelines for RNA structural analyses

</details>

## Teaching

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Research Mentor</strong></summary>

-   Amgen Scholars Program
-   UCLA

</details>

<details class="cv-drop">

<summary><strong>Teaching Assistant</strong></summary>

-   Preparation for Research in Computational Biology
-   Introduction to Laboratory and Scientific Methodology

</details>

[Back to top](#top)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
